title: PluginManifest
description: Plugin registration manifest for the Unified AI Plugin Platform. Describes plugin identity, runtime requirements, capabilities, commands, AI integration, and optional supported_platforms declaration (v1.1.0+) used by hosting platforms such as Portunix Synapse to discover and embed extension plugins. This is the JSON Schema equivalent of the core PluginService.GetInfo gRPC response (portunix/src/app/plugins/proto/plugin.proto).

name.description: Plugin identifier used in CLI (e.g. 'reco', 'scraper', 'vox'). Must be unique across the ecosystem.

type.description: Plugin execution model: helper (CLI dispatch), grpc (long-running service), executable (standalone)

binary.description: Path to binary, JAR, or entry point (e.g. 'ptx-reco', 'fulltext-1.0.8.jar')

runtime_version.description: Minimum runtime version (e.g. '>=21' for Java, '>=3.11' for Python)

python_min_version.description: Minimum Python version (shorthand for runtime_version when runtime is python)

python_version.description: Exact Python interpreter minor the plugin was built for, e.g. '3.13'. Required for bytecode-only wheels whose .pyc is tied to a specific minor. When set, the host provisions this exact interpreter (via uv) for the plugin venv; distinct from python_min_version, which is only a compatibility floor.

mode.description: Execution mode: helper (run and exit) or service (long-running)

health_check_interval.description: Health check interval in nanoseconds (for service mode)

interfaces.description: Exposed interfaces. A plugin can combine multiple (e.g. ['cli','grpc'] for dual-mode plugins). When omitted, inferred from type/mode for backward compatibility.

optional_tools.description: External tools that enhance plugin functionality

mcp_tools.description: MCP tools exposed by this plugin

commands.description: CLI commands provided by this plugin

supported_platforms.description: Declaration of hosting platforms this plugin extends (e.g. 'synapse', 'pack', 'agent'). Added in schema v1.1.0. Omit for plugins that do not extend a higher-level platform. Consumers (platforms) query ptx plugin list --platform=<name> and receive matching plugins together with their platform_payload for embedding. Portunix validates the shape of each entry but treats platform_payload as opaque — its content is owned and validated by the target platform.

name.description: Canonical platform identifier this plugin targets (e.g. 'synapse', 'pack', 'agent'). Lowercase, short token.

min_version.description: Minimum platform SemVer this plugin is compatible with (inclusive). Omit for no lower bound. Format: '<major>.<minor>.<patch>' (pre-release suffixes accepted).

max_version.description: Maximum platform SemVer this plugin is compatible with (inclusive). Omit for no upper bound. Format: '<major>.<minor>.<patch>' (pre-release suffixes accepted).

features.description: Short capability tokens used for capability negotiation without parsing platform_payload. Consumers may filter plugins with --feature=<token> (AND-filter). Examples: 'connector.command', 'assistant.chat'.

platform_payload.description: Opaque, platform-specific registration payload. Portunix validates only that this is a JSON object and stores it byte-identical for round-trip delivery to the platform. Content validation is the platform owner's responsibility (e.g. Synapse validates against synapse-extension-manifest.schema.json). Keep plugin-sensitive data out of this field — it is visible to any caller that queries by platform.
